item.title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
item.description: European Central Bank supervisors are set to warn bankers вҖӢabout the risks posed by вҖҢAnthropic's new artificial intelligence model that might supercharge cyberattacks, one source familiar вҖӢwith the situation told Reuters.

item.title: Allbirds shares jump over 400% on plans to pivot to AI from sneakers
item.description: Shares of Allbirds surged more than five-fold on Wednesday after the footwear maker said it was raising capital and pivoting towards AI computing infrastructure.

item.title: Big ad agencies settle US FTC probe into alleged boycott over political content
item.description: Three large advertising agencies вҖӢsettled a U.S. Federal Trade Commission probe accusing them of violating вҖҢantitrust law by conspiring to boycott online media platforms based on political content they didn't like, the agency said on Wednesday.

item.title: Middle East war damage to energy assets may cost up to $58 billion, research firm Rystad says
item.description: The Middle East conflict could saddle the region with as much as $58 billion in repair costs for energy-linked infrastructure, with oil вҖӢand gas facilities alone accounting for up to $50 billion, according to вҖҢa report by Rystad Energy.

item.title: BYD shuns price war in South Africa to build EV brand
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD вҖҢis deliberately avoiding a price war in South Africa as competition ramps up in the continent's second biggest auto market, its local managing director told Reuters on Wednesday.

item.title: EU warns Meta WhatsApp AI fee breaches antitrust rules, orders rollback
item.description: The European Commission said on Wednesday it intended to order Meta Platforms to reinstate rival artificial intelligence assistants on its WhatsApp messaging service after the U.S. вҖӢtech giant imposed an access fee.

item.title: Next head of UK's PRA says AI threats and opportunities should be addressed equally
item.description: The incoming head of the UK's prudential вҖӢregulator for banks and вҖҢinsurers said on Wednesday risks and opportunities arising from вҖӢthe widespread adoption вҖӢof artificial intelligence in the вҒ financial sector should вҖӢbe addressed equally.

item.title: US can't wait forever to bring down fiscal deficits, IMF says
item.description: The United States needs to reduce its fiscal deficit вҖӢby about four percentage points of вҖҢGDP from the current level of 7% and needs a robust, credible plan вҖӢto do so, International Monetary вҖӢFund Fiscal Affairs Director Rodrigo Valdes вҒ said on Wednesday.

item.title: US home builder sentiment drops to seven-month low in April, NAHB survey says
item.description: U.S. homebuilder sentiment deteriorated in April, hitting a seven-month low as the war with Iran led to higher prices for materials and mortgage rates as well as increased economic uncertainty, a вҖӢsurvey showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Pizza chains Papa John's and Pizza Hut edge closer to finding new owners, sources say
item.description: Two of America's most popular national pizza chains are edging closer to selling to new owners as stiff competition, rising commodity costs and waning consumer demand are undercutting вҖӢperformance, according to five people familiar with the discussions.

item.title: Fed's Hammack: Rates likely on hold 'for a good while' - CNBC
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said on Wednesday that while she sees no imminent need for the вҖҢcentral bank to change its interest rate target setting, itвҖҷs possible cuts or even hikes could lie ahead.

item.title: UK financial watchdog to consult on proposed crypto regulations
item.description: Britain's financial regulator said on вҖӢWednesday it was вҖҢconsulting on how firms might be вҖӢaffected by вҖӢthe regulation of the вҒ crypto industry which вҖӢis due to вҖӢcome into effect by October 2027.

item.title: American Eagle rallies again after launching second Sydney Sweeney campaign
item.description: American Eagle Outfitters shares jumped on Wednesday after the denim retailer unveiled a second campaign with "Euphoria" star Sydney Sweeney, вҖӢa year after a viral and controversial ad featuring вҖҢthe actress fueled a stock rally.

item.title: Adobe releases AI assistant for creative tools, says it will work with Anthropic's Claude
item.description: Adobe said on Wednesday it was releasing a new artificial intelligence assistant designed to help users carry out tasks вҖӢacross its suite of software for editing photos, videos and other вҖҢdigital content.

item.title: IMF cautions countries against broad fuel subsidies to deal with war-driven energy shock
item.description: The war in the Middle East has intensified strains on an already fragile global fiscal situation, with higher interest rates and rising energy prices already fueling calls for support from emerging markets and developing economies, the International Monetary Fund said on Wednesday in its вҖӢFiscal Monitor report.

item.title: Jane Street signs $6 billion AI cloud deal with CoreWeave, boosts stake
item.description: Trading firm Jane Street has committed about $6 billion for CoreWeave's cloud services, marking the third major deal for the вҖӢNvidia-backed neocloud company in a week and underscoring surging demand вҖҢfor computing capacity.

item.title: Economic growth will be slower this quarter amid war, US Treasury chief says
item.description: U.S. Treasury вҖӢSecretary Scott Bessent said on вҖҢWednesday that the economy will be slower this quarter amid the вҖӢIran war, but it вҖӢis in good shape and вҒ will rebound, adding that вҖӢoil prices do not appear вҖӢto be weighing on inflation expectations.

item.title: China's CATL beats first quarter forecasts, boosting market dominance
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle battery giant CATL posted first-quarter results that surpassed expectations on Wednesday, strengthening its market вҖӢdominance even as EV demand softens.

item.title: Silver faces sixth year of deficit with stock drawdown raising squeeze risks, research shows
item.description: The silver market is heading for a вҖҢsixth year of structural deficit, with 762 million troy ounces drawn from stocks since 2021, raising the risk of a renewed liquidity squeeze despite weaker demand expectations, the Silver Institute and consultancy Metals Focus said on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump backs government AI safeguards in banking system, acknowledges risks
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump acknowledged the risks вҖӢartificial intelligence posed to the banking system вҖҢand said there should be government safeguards, but also believed the technology could make the вҖӢbanking system better and safer, according вҖӢto an interview broadcast on Wednesday.

item.title: US Treasury asks private credit firms for data, Punchbowl News reports
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department вҖӢhas begun asking вҖҢprivate credit firms to submit information вҖӢabout their вҖӢbusiness models and ties вҒ to the вҖӢregulated financial system, вҖӢPunchbowl News reported on Wednesday citing sources вҖӢfamiliar with вҖӢthe matter.

item.title: Nutella-maker Ferrero aware of EU inspections, says it is cooperating
item.description: Nutella-maker Ferrero said on Wednesday вҖӢthat the company is вҖҢaware that on-site inspections are currently taking place вҖӢin its offices вҖӢby European Commission officials.

item.title: Morgan Stanley's profit beats estimates on dealmaking surge, trading boost
item.description: Morgan Stanley beat Wall Street expectations for first-quarter profit on Wednesday, as вҖӢthe investment bank benefited from a surge in dealmaking and raked in record revenue from its вҖҢequities trading business.

item.title: Robots, drones could slash global food delivery costs to $1 per order, Barclays says
item.description: Autonomous food delivery robots and drones could cut costs by several dollars to as low as $1 per order, a shift вҖӢthat could unlock billions of dollars in profits for вҖҢthe global food delivery industry, Barclays said on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump threatens to fire Powell if he doesn't leave Fed board; probe to continue
item.description: President Donald вҖҢTrump on Wednesday threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell from his separate seat as a member of the U.S. central bank's Board of Governors if Powell does not vacate that post вҖӢas well when his term as Fed chief ends on May 15, intensifying a вҖӢcomplicated standoff that has upended the Fed's usually smooth transition of вҒ power.

item.title: Leidos, Analogic to form security tech joint venture
item.description: Defense contractor Leidos Holdings will spin off its security and industrial automation businesses and вҖӢcombine them with security imaging and вҖҢdetection company Analogic Corporation to form a new joint venture, the companies said on Wednesday.

item.title: Recruiter Robert Walters posts smaller fee drop as hiring improves
item.description: Recruitment firm Robert Walters on Wednesday вҖҢposted a narrower drop in first-quarter net fees following double-digit quarterly declines for nearly three years, as its largest market Japan returned to growth.

item.title: Walmart redesigns its 'Great Value' private label brand
item.description: Walmart said on Wednesday it is redesigning its private-label brand "Great Value" вҖӢas price-conscious shoppers increasingly opt for вҖҢcheaper store-brand products.

item.title: Bank of America profit rises as trading, investment banking shine
item.description: Bank of America's profit rose in the first quarter, as volatility in the global markets lifted trading activity and a rebound in mergers and acquisitions boosted the lender's вҖӢinvestment banking fees.

item.title: Morning Bid: Back to business
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today
